Notice Of Petition And Petition To Compel Arbitration And Stay Judicial Proceedings
Off Calendar Jul-08-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Benjamin Karpilow, Attorney at Law, Tel. 707.528.4409, for plaintiffs;
Daryl S. Landy, of Morgan, Lewis & Bockius LLP, Tel. 650.843.7561, for defendants.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT SPLUNK INC.'S Petition To Compel Arbitration And Stay Judicial Proceedings is granted. Plaintiff and Defendant entered into a binding arbitration agreement that provides that Plaintiff is required to arbitrate the causes of action in the complaint. (Declaration of Evangelina Vallez, Ex. A, Employment Agreement, par. 8.) Plaintiff's statements that he was required to sign the arbitration agreement as a condition of his employment and was not given an opportunity to negotiate its terms are insufficient to render the agreement procedurally unconscionable. (See Trend Homes, Inc. v. Sup. Ct. (2005) 131 Cal.App.4th 950, 958; see also Lagatree v. Luce, Forward, Hamilton and Scripps (1999) 74 Cal.App.4th 1105, 1127.) Additionally, failure to provide the AAA Rules, without more, is insufficient to render the agreement procedurally unconscionable. (See Peng v. First Republic Bank (2013) 219 Cal.App.4th 1462, 1470.) Because Plaintiff has failed to establish procedural unconscionability, the agreement is enforceable. (See Fitz v. NCR Corp. (2004) 118 Cal.App.4th 702, 714, "Both procedural and substantive elements of unconscionability must be present for a court to refuse to enforce an arbitration agreement.") The litigation is stayed pending the conclusion of arbitration. Plaintiff may adjudicate his claims for equitable relief in court once the litigation stay is lifted. Order signed in open court. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302. (302/EHG)
